---
description: Complete auto-generated reference for all AI-Hydro MCP tools, grouped by tier and domain, with parameters and worked examples.
---

# Complete Tool Reference

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by ai_hydro.mcp.tools_docs.generate_tool_reference — do not edit by hand. Run `python -m ai_hydro.mcp.tools_docs` to refresh. -->

This page lists **all 113 tools** registered on aihydro-tools `v1.7.0`, exactly as the agent sees them. Tools are grouped by **tier** (how readily they are surfaced to the agent) and **domain**.

- **113 tools** total — **26 hot** (full schema always in-context), the rest fetched on demand via `describe_tool(name)`.
- Call `list_available_tools()` at runtime for the live count on your installation; community plugins add more.

---

## Tier 1 — Core (always in-context)

### Claims & Provenance

#### `add_assumption` · :material-fire: hot

Record a scientific assumption or caveat in the session ledger.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `assumption_id` | string | yes |  |
| `statement` | string | yes |  |
| `risk` | string | yes |  |
| `risk_rationale` | string | yes |  |
| `affects` | array | yes |  |
| `scope` | any | — |  |

```python
add_assumption(session_id='01646500', assumption_id='...', statement='...', risk='...', risk_rationale='...', affects=[])
```

#### `add_claim` · :material-fire: hot

Add a scoped scientific claim to the session ledger.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `claim_id` | string | yes |  |
| `statement` | string | yes |  |
| `claim_type` | string | yes |  |
| `status` | string | yes |  |
| `confidence` | string | yes |  |
| `confidence_rationale` | string | yes |  |
| `basins` | array | yes |  |
| `period` | string | yes |  |
| `metric` | any | — |  |
| `limitations` | any | — |  |
| `evidence` | any | — |  |
| `evidence_spans` | any | — |  |

```python
add_claim(session_id='01646500', claim_id='...', statement='...', claim_type='...', status='...', confidence='...', confidence_rationale='...', basins=[], period='...')
```

#### `draft_claim_from_run` · :material-fire: hot

Draft a claim pre-bound to evidence from a Tier 1 run. Reads session._run_log[run_id], returns a template with evidence_spans filled in. Agent authors only the 'statement' and 'confidence_rationale'. run_id: from a Tier 1 tool's _run_id field. metric_ref: e.g. kge, runoff_ratio, baseflow_index.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `run_id` | string | yes |  |
| `metric_ref` | string | yes |  |
| `claim_id` | any | — |  |

```python
draft_claim_from_run(session_id='01646500', run_id='...', metric_ref='...')
```

#### `promote_claim_to_registry` · :material-fire: hot

Promote a session claim to the global knowledge registry. Requires researcher approval and passes through a strict validation gate.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `claim_id` | string | yes |  |
| `researcher_approved` | boolean | — |  |

```python
promote_claim_to_registry(session_id='01646500', claim_id='...')
```

### Modelling

#### `get_model_results` · :material-fire: hot

Return cached training results: NSE, KGE, RMSE, model_dir. job_id reads from the job artifact dir; otherwise from session.model. session_id : str | None (optional from Wave 3) — auto-resolved from chat context.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `job_id` | any | — |  |

```python
get_model_results(session_id='01646500', job_id='...')
```

#### `train_hydro_model` · :material-fire: hot

Kick off a model training job (detached subprocess). Returns {job_id} immediately — poll with get_training_status (≤1× per minute). Requires watershed+forcing (HBV) or watershed+streamflow+forcing (LSTM). framework: hbv (HBV-light, default) | neuralhydrology (LSTM, needs pip install neuralhydrology). model (NH only): cudalstm | ealstm | transformer. Typical runtime: 2-15 min (HBV), 15-60 min (LSTM).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `workspace_dir` | any | — |  |
| `framework` | string | — |  |
| `model` | string | — |  |
| `train_start` | string | — |  |
| `train_end` | string | — |  |
| `val_start` | string | — |  |
| `val_end` | string | — |  |
| `test_start` | string | — |  |
| `test_end` | string | — |  |
| `epochs` | integer | — |  |
| `n_restarts` | integer | — |  |
| `hidden_size` | integer | — |  |
| `learning_rate` | number | — |  |

```python
train_hydro_model(session_id='01646500', workspace_dir='...')
```

### Streamflow

#### `extract_hydrological_signatures` · :material-fire: hot

Extract 17 CAMELS-style hydrological signatures (flow stats, BFI, runoff ratio, elasticity, high/low flow events, FDC slope, timing). Requires delineate_watershed first. Defaults to CAMELS analysis period 1989-10-01 to 2009-09-30.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `start_date` | string | — |  |
| `end_date` | string | — |  |
| `geometry_geojson` | any | — |  |

```python
extract_hydrological_signatures(session_id='01646500', start_date='1989-10-01')
```

#### `separate_baseflow` · :material-fire: hot

Separate baseflow from streamflow via digital filter. Writes daily series + BFI to session.baseflow. Requires fetch_streamflow_data first.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `method` | string | — |  |
| `alpha` | number | — |  |
| `n_passes` | integer | — |  |

```python
separate_baseflow(session_id='01646500', method='lyne_hollick')
```

### Consistency Validators

#### `check_temporal_alignment` · :material-fire: hot

Check if two time-series slots share the same temporal range.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `slot_a` | string | yes |  |
| `slot_b` | string | yes |  |

```python
check_temporal_alignment(session_id='01646500', slot_a='...', slot_b='...')
```

#### `check_unit_consistency` · :material-fire: hot

Check if a session slot uses the expected scientific units.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `slot` | string | yes |  |
| `expected_units` | string | yes |  |

```python
check_unit_consistency(session_id='01646500', slot='...', expected_units='...')
```

#### `check_water_balance_consistency` · :material-fire: hot

Check if annual runoff is less than or equal to annual precipitation.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |

```python
check_water_balance_consistency(session_id='01646500')
```

### Watershed & Terrain

#### `compute_twi` · :material-fire: hot

Topographic Wetness Index TWI = ln(a / tan(beta)). Used for soil moisture mapping, saturated zone ID, runoff generation. Works globally: USGS 3DEP inside CONUS, Copernicus GLO-30 everywhere else. Requires delineate_watershed or delineate_watershed_from_point first. Saves GeoTIFF + PNG + HTML map when workspace_dir is set and create_map=True.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `resolution` | integer | — |  |
| `create_map` | boolean | — |  |
| `geometry_geojson` | any | — |  |

```python
compute_twi(session_id='01646500', resolution=30)
```

#### `create_cn_grid` · :material-fire: hot

NRCS Curve Number grid: NLCD land cover × Polaris soil → distributed CN. Currently CONUS only (NLCD + POLARIS coverage). Global CN grid (ESA WorldCover + SoilGrids) is planned for a future release. Requires delineate_watershed first. Returns CN stats, zone percentages, LULC + soil breakdowns. Saves GeoTIFF / NetCDF / PNG / HTML. year: NLCD year (default 2019).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `year` | integer | — |  |
| `resolution` | integer | — |  |
| `create_map` | boolean | — |  |
| `geometry_geojson` | any | — |  |

```python
create_cn_grid(session_id='01646500', year=2019)
```

#### `delineate_watershed` · :material-fire: hot

Delineate USGS gauge watershed via NLDI + NWIS metadata.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `gauge_id` | any | — |  |
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `workspace_dir` | any | — |  |

```python
delineate_watershed(gauge_id='01646500', session_id='01646500')
```

#### `delineate_watershed_from_point` · :material-fire: hot

Delineate a watershed from a pour point (lat/lon, EPSG:4326). Tiered: NLDI in CONUS, MERIT Hydro via GEE + pyflwdir globally, local MERIT rasters if requested, then raw DEM fallback / MERIT-Basins expert tiers.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `lat` | number | yes |  |
| `lon` | number | yes |  |
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `workspace_dir` | any | — |  |
| `expected_area_km2` | any | — |  |
| `method` | string | — |  |
| `name` | any | — |  |

```python
delineate_watershed_from_point(lat=28.22, lon=76.77)
```

#### `extract_geomorphic_parameters` · :material-fire: hot

Extract 28 geomorphic parameters (morphometry, relief, drainage network, shape indices) from a 30m DEM. Works globally: uses USGS 3DEP inside CONUS and Copernicus GLO-30 via aihydro-data everywhere else. Requires delineate_watershed or delineate_watershed_from_point first. Returns DA_km2, Lp_km, Lb_km, shape indices, hypsometric integral, drainage density, stream-order metrics, etc.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `dem_resolution` | integer | — |  |
| `geometry_geojson` | any | — |  |

```python
extract_geomorphic_parameters(session_id='01646500', dem_resolution=30)
```

## Tier 2 — Extended

### Analysis

#### `compute_spectral_index` · :material-fire: hot

Compute a spectral index (NDWI, NDVI, NDBI, NBR, MNDWI, …) for the current study watershed.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `index_name` | string | yes |  |
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `start` | any | — |  |
| `end` | any | — |  |
| `sensor` | string | — |  |
| `frequency` | any | — |  |
| `mask_clouds` | boolean | — |  |
| `create_map` | boolean | — |  |
| `native_resolution` | boolean | — |  |

```python
compute_spectral_index(index_name='NDWI')
```

### CAMELS Attributes

#### `fetch_camels_us` · :material-fire: hot

CAMELS-US static catchment attributes (671 minimally-disturbed CONUS gauges). Single gauge via ``gauge_id`` (cached in session) OR bulk via ``gauge_ids`` (list, [] = all 671; saved to workspace). Returns ~60 attributes grouped by topography/climate/hydrology/soil/ vegetation/geology. Citation: Addor et al. (2017), HESS 21. session_id : str | None (optional from Wave 3) — auto-resolved from chat context.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `gauge_id` | any | — |  |
| `gauge_ids` | any | — |  |

```python
fetch_camels_us(session_id='01646500', gauge_id='01646500')
```

### Citations & Literature

#### `index_literature`

Scan a folder of papers (.txt, .md, .pdf via pypdf) and build a text-only searchable index (literature_index.md with first ~800 chars per doc). Re-run when papers are added. folder_path defaults to ~/.aihydro/projects/<name>/literature/.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_name` | string | yes |  |
| `folder_path` | any | — |  |

```python
index_literature(project_name='...')
```

#### `lookup_citation`

Look up a citation (free text or DOI). Cascade: CrossRef → Semantic Scholar → DataCite. Cached at ~/.aihydro/citations/ (30 day TTL).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `query` | string | yes |  |
| `source_hint` | any | — |  |
| `force_refresh` | boolean | — |  |

```python
lookup_citation(query='...')
```

#### `search_literature`

Query the literature index. Text match on filenames + excerpts, no vector DB. return_full_content=True returns full text of matched docs for the LLM to synthesise (can be large).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_name` | string | yes |  |
| `query` | string | yes |  |
| `return_full_content` | boolean | — |  |

```python
search_literature(project_name='...', query='...')
```

```python
update_claim_status(session_id='01646500', claim_id='...', status='...', confidence='...', rationale='...')
```

### Course / Teaching

#### `course_navigate`

Push the HTML Preview panel to open a specific course module.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `module_id` | string | yes |  |
| `reason` | any | — |  |

```python
course_navigate(module_id='...')
```

#### `course_scaffold`

Scaffold a course on disk: course.json + one styled module.html per module (AI-Hydro design + edit-mode markers). Load the ``course-authoring`` skill first for the full workflow.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `title` | string | yes |  |
| `out_dir` | string | yes |  |
| `modules` | array | yes |  |
| `course_id` | any | — |  |
| `abstract` | any | — |  |
| `estimated_hours` | any | — |  |
| `author_name` | string | — |  |
| `author_affiliation` | string | — |  |
| `topic` | string | — |  |
| `level` | string | — |  |
| `overwrite` | boolean | — |  |

```python
course_scaffold(title='...', out_dir='...', modules=[])
```

#### `course_set_progress`

Mutate progress for the active course. Requires explicit user agreement.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `module_id` | string | yes |  |
| `action` | string | — |  |
| `reason` | any | — |  |

```python
course_set_progress(module_id='...')
```

### Data Fetch (aihydro-data)

#### `data_batch_fetch`

Parallel fetch over N geometries (e.g. a set of watersheds or gauges).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `variable` | string | yes |  |
| `geometries` | array | yes |  |
| `start` | string | yes |  |
| `end` | string | yes |  |
| `labels` | any | — |  |
| `mode` | string | — |  |
| `product` | any | — |  |
| `aggregation` | string | — |  |
| `max_workers` | integer | — |  |
| `on_error` | string | — |  |

```python
data_batch_fetch(variable='...', geometries=[], start='2020-01-01', end='2020-12-31')
```

#### `data_describe_product`

Return the full ProductSpec for a single product, including citation, BibTeX, and agent-facing next_steps.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `product_id` | string | yes |  |

```python
data_describe_product(product_id='...')
```

#### `data_fetch` · :material-fire: hot

Fetch a single hydrology variable for one geometry / time window.

|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `variable` | string | yes |  |
| `geometry` | any | yes |  |
| `start` | string | yes |  |
| `end` | string | yes |  |
| `mode` | string | — |  |
| `product` | any | — |  |
| `aggregation` | string | — |  |
| `cache` | boolean | — |  |

```python
data_fetch(variable='...', geometry='...', start='2020-01-01', end='2020-12-31')
```

Discover available data products, optionally filtered.

```python
data_list_products(variable='...', region='...')
```

### Execution

#### `run_python` · :material-fire: hot

Execute a Python snippet in a sandboxed subprocess inside workspace_dir.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `script` | string | yes |  |
| `workspace_dir` | string | yes |  |
| `timeout_seconds` | integer | — |  |
| `allow_network` | boolean | — |  |

```python
run_python(script='...', workspace_dir='...')
```

### General

#### `get_citation_by_doi`

Resolve a citation by exact DOI (uses cache when fresh). Same schema as lookup_citation.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `doi` | string | yes |  |
| `force_refresh` | boolean | — |  |

```python
get_citation_by_doi(doi='...')
```

#### `write_research_interpretation`

Phase 2 of interpretation: persist LLM-authored scientific synthesis to research.md + session so it's auto-injected into every future conversation. interpretation: 3-6 sentences of flowing prose (no bullets). site_name: short descriptive slug for display + exports.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `site_name` | string | — |  |
| `interpretation` | string | — |  |

```python
write_research_interpretation(session_id='01646500', site_name='')
```

### Research Ledger

#### `add_journal_entry`

Add a timestamped entry to the project's experiment journal (persistent across conversations; searchable via search_experiments). Use for findings, decisions, hypotheses, anomalies, lit-synthesis conclusions.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_name` | string | yes |  |
| `entry` | string | yes |  |
| `tags` | any | — |  |

```python
add_journal_entry(project_name='...', entry='...')
```

#### `search_experiments`

Full-text search across all sessions in a project (case-insensitive over every computed slot's JSON). Use for "find basins with NSE > 0.7", "which gauges have high BFI". compare_sessions=True adds a side-by-side metrics table.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_name` | string | yes |  |
| `query` | string | yes |  |
| `compare_sessions` | boolean | — |  |

```python
search_experiments(project_name='...', query='...')
```

#### `map_apply_symbology`

Apply graduated (choropleth) symbology to an existing vector layer on the map.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `layer_id` | string | yes |  |
| `attribute` | string | yes |  |
| `method` | string | — |  |
| `num_classes` | integer | — |  |
| `color_ramp` | string | — |  |

```python
map_apply_symbology(layer_id='...', attribute='...')
```

#### `map_save_roi`

Save the host map session active ROI to workspace roi/<slug>.geojson and update roi/active.json. Requires an active ROI on the map session file.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `name` | string | yes |  |
| `workspace_dir` | any | — |  |

```python
map_save_roi(session_id='01646500', name='...')
```

#### `map_set_roi`

Set the active map ROI (study basin polygon). The map panel updates via the host command watcher. Pass GeoJSON Feature, FeatureCollection, or JSON string.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `geojson` | any | yes |  |
| `name` | string | — |  |
| `area_ha` | number | — |  |

```python
map_set_roi(session_id='01646500', geojson='...')
```

#### `map_set_working_geometry`

Set which workspace GeoJSON file is the active study geometry for this session (used by current_map_basin / GEE). Path is relative to workspace, e.g. vectors/my_basin.geojson or watershed_01031500.geojson.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `workspace_path` | string | yes |  |

```python
map_set_working_geometry(session_id='01646500', workspace_path='...')
```

#### `map_update_layer`

Update layer style/visibility in-place (don't rewrite GeoJSON for styling). For choropleth, set graduated_attribute (+ method/classes/ramp).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `layer_id` | string | yes |  |
| `style_preset` | any | — |  |
| `fill_color` | any | — |  |
| `stroke_color` | any | — |  |
| `fill_opacity` | any | — |  |
| `stroke_width` | any | — |  |
| `visible` | any | — |  |
| `display_name` | any | — |  |
| `raster_colormap` | any | — |  |
| `raster_opacity` | any | — |  |
| `clear_graduated` | boolean | — |  |
| `graduated_attribute` | any | — |  |
| `graduated_method` | string | — |  |
| `graduated_classes` | integer | — |  |
| `color_ramp` | string | — |  |

```python
map_update_layer(layer_id='...')
```

### Researcher Persona

#### `log_researcher_observation`

Log a meaningful observation about the researcher (memory-style; for durable inferences not captured by structured profile fields). Skip trivial confirmations or temporary preferences.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `observation` | string | yes |  |

```python
log_researcher_observation(observation='...')
```

### HTML Preview

#### `preview_address_comment`

Address a user comment. new_text (optional) proposes replacement text for the commented selection — user sees a diff. Omit to just resolve.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `module_id` | string | yes |  |
| `comment_id` | string | yes |  |
| `new_text` | any | — |  |

```python
preview_address_comment(module_id='...', comment_id='...')
```

#### `preview_revise_section`

Propose a revised HTML section (well-formed snippet, no <html>/<body>). User sees a diff to accept/reject. Used in the edit-mode comment flow.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `module_id` | string | yes |  |
| `section_id` | string | yes |  |
| `new_html` | string | yes |  |

```python
preview_revise_section(module_id='...', section_id='...', new_html='...')
```

### Session Management

#### `add_note`

Append a researcher annotation (hypothesis, anomaly, decision) to the session.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `note` | string | — |  |

```python
add_note(session_id='01646500', note='')
```

#### `export_session`

Export the session. format='capsule' (default) writes a reproducible research folder (README.md + methods.md + citations.bib + environment.yml + session.json + data/figures/model/); 'bibtex' or 'json' write a single file. capsule_path: override default location.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `capsule_path` | any | — |  |
| `format` | string | — |  |

```python
export_session(session_id='01646500', capsule_path='...')
```

### Watershed & Terrain

#### `merit_add_map_layers`

Build MERIT vector layers for the map viewport and optionally push to the map panel.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `lat` | number | yes |  |
| `lon` | number | yes |  |
| `min_lon` | any | — |  |
| `min_lat` | any | — |  |
| `max_lon` | any | — |  |
| `max_lat` | any | — |  |
| `include_catchments` | boolean | — |  |
| `push_to_map` | boolean | — |  |

```python
merit_add_map_layers(lat=28.22, lon=76.77)
```

#### `merit_ensure_basin`

Ensure MERIT-Hydro vectors exist for the Pfaf basin at (lat, lon) WGS84. Auto-downloads if AIHYDRO_MERIT_BASE_URL is set. Returns readiness flags.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `lat` | number | yes |  |
| `lon` | number | yes |  |
| `download` | boolean | — |  |

```python
merit_ensure_basin(lat=28.22, lon=76.77)
```

#### `merit_ensure_basins_region`

Check or stage regional MERIT-Basins vector/topology assets for hybrid routing.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `pfaf_region` | string | yes |  |
| `acquisition_policy` | string | — |  |

```python
merit_ensure_basins_region(pfaf_region='...')
```

#### `merit_ensure_region`

Ensure MERIT river vectors for all Pfaf basins in a named region preset.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `preset` | string | yes |  |
| `lat` | any | — |  |
| `lon` | any | — |  |
| `download` | boolean | — |  |

```python
merit_ensure_region(preset='...')
```

#### `merit_ensure_routing_region`

Check or stage flowdir-first regional MERIT routing assets.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `lat` | any | — |  |
| `lon` | any | — |  |
| `pfaf_region` | any | — |  |
| `acquisition_policy` | string | — |  |

```python
merit_ensure_routing_region(lat=28.22, lon=76.77)
```

---

## Tier 3 — Specialist

### Analysis

#### `list_spectral_indices`

List all available spectral indices, their required bands, colormaps, citations, and recommended use cases.

_No parameters._

```python
list_spectral_indices()
```

### Claims & Provenance

#### `list_assumptions`

List all assumptions in the session.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `validated` | any | — |  |

```python
list_assumptions(session_id='01646500')
```

#### `list_claims`

List all scientific claims in the session.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |
| `status` | any | — |  |

```python
list_claims(session_id='01646500')
```

### Course / Teaching

#### `course_get_curriculum`

Full manifest + prerequisite_graph for a course. Use when you need the structure (not just progress). ``course_id_or_path``: courseId of the active course OR absolute path to course.json. Defaults to active course.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `course_id_or_path` | any | — |  |

```python
course_get_curriculum(course_id_or_path='...')
```

#### `course_get_state`

Snapshot of the active course: current module, completion %, locked modules, next_recommended id. Call at the start of any course conversation. Returns {active: false} if no course is open.

_No parameters._

```python
course_get_state()
```

### Data Fetch (aihydro-data)

#### `data_doctor`

Environment health check — probes each backend, auth state, cache size, and version compatibility. Agents call this when something goes wrong to figure out *what* is wrong before guessing.

_No parameters._

```python
data_doctor()
```

#### `data_get_cache_status`

Return a summary of the disk cache at ~/.aihydro/cache/data/.

_No parameters._

```python
data_get_cache_status()
```

#### `data_help`

Guided onboarding and topic reference for aihydro-data.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `topic` | any | — |  |

```python
data_help(topic='...')
```

#### `data_invalidate_cache`

Remove a specific entry from the disk cache.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `cache_key` | string | yes |  |

```python
data_invalidate_cache(cache_key='...')
```

### Discovery

#### `aihydro_describe_capability` · :material-fire: hot

Return a focused 1-line-per-tool summary of tools relevant to a domain.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `domain` | any | — |  |

```python
aihydro_describe_capability(domain='...')
```

#### `describe_tool` · :material-fire: hot

Fetch the FULL parameter schema for a single tool, plus a worked example.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | yes |  |

```python
describe_tool(name='...')
```

#### `describe_tools` · :material-fire: hot

Fetch full parameter schemas for several tools at once (batch describe_tool).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `names` | array | yes |  |

```python
describe_tools(names=[])
```

#### `list_available_tools` · :material-fire: hot

List all registered MCP tools (built-in + community plugins via the aihydro.tools entry point). For token-efficient discovery, prefer aihydro_describe_capability(domain) over this full dump.

_No parameters._

```python
list_available_tools()
```

### Execution

#### `get_library_reference`

Look up field-name gotchas, API quirks, and copy-paste patterns for a Python library used in hydrological workflows.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `library` | any | — |  |

```python
get_library_reference(library='...')
```

#### `list_relevant_clis`

List installed AI-Hydro-aware CLIs (registered via aihydro.clis entry-point, plus aihydro-mcp). Use before driving any domain CLI from a shell.

_No parameters._

```python
list_relevant_clis()
```

### Meteorological Forcing

#### `fetch_forcing_data`

[DEPRECATED — prefer data_fetch] Basin-averaged daily forcing data. Globally aware: - **CONUS**: GridMET (pr, tmmx, tmmn, srad, vs, rmax, rmin, pet, erc). - **Global**: CHIRPS precipitation + ERA5-Land temperature via aihydro-data.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `start_date` | string | yes |  |
| `end_date` | string | yes |  |
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `variables` | any | — |  |
| `geometry_geojson` | any | — |  |

```python
fetch_forcing_data(start_date='2020-01-01', end_date='2020-12-31')
```

### General

#### `aihydro_chat_status`

Show what study (if any) is currently bound to this chat.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `chat_id` | any | — |  |

```python
aihydro_chat_status(chat_id='...')
```

#### `aihydro_rebind_chat`

Rebind the current chat to a specific existing study.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `study_id` | string | yes |  |
| `chat_id` | any | — |  |

```python
aihydro_rebind_chat(study_id='...')
```

#### `list_cached_citations`

List cached DOIs in ~/.aihydro/citations/. No API calls.

_No parameters._

```python
list_cached_citations()
```

```python
get_dataset_info(dataset_id='...')
```

```python
get_equation_definition(equation_id='...')
```

```python
get_metric_definition(metric_id='...')
```

```python
get_variable_definition(variable_id='...')
```

```python
list_known_datasets(domain='...', workspace_dir='...')
```

Return all known metrics, optionally filtered by domain.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |

```python
list_known_metrics(domain='...', workspace_dir='...')
```

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_id` | any | — |  |

```python
gee.status(project_id='...')
```

#### `map_fit_extent`

Ask the map to fit the viewport to the active ROI or visible layers.

_No parameters._

```python
map_fit_extent()
```

Zoom the map viewport to a layer's extent.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `layer_id` | string | yes |  |

```python
map_fit_layer(layer_id='...')
```

#### `map_get_state`

Map session snapshot from ~/.aihydro/map_session.json: basemap, view, visible layers, workspace root, active ROI, recent events.

|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `event_limit` | integer | — |  |

```python
map_get_state(session_id='01646500', event_limit=10)
```

#### `map_list_layers`

List map layer ids and catalog entries from the host (~/.aihydro/map_layer_catalog.json).

_No parameters._

```python
map_list_layers()
```

#### `map_remove_layer`

Remove a layer from the map by id.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `layer_id` | string | yes |  |

```python
map_remove_layer(layer_id='...')
```

| `basemap_name` | any | — |  |

```python
map_set_basemap(basemap_id='...')
```

#### `map_show`

Open the AI-Hydro map panel if it is not already visible.

_No parameters._

```python
map_show()
```

Push any GeoJSON geometry directly onto the AI-Hydro map panel.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `geojson` | string | yes |  |

| `fill_color` | any | — |  |

```python
show_on_map(geojson='...')
```

### Modelling

#### `get_training_status`

Poll the status of a training job started by train_hydro_model.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `job_id` | string | yes |  |

```python
get_training_status(job_id='...')
```

### Researcher Persona

#### `get_researcher_profile`

Persistent researcher profile (domain, tools, preferences, focus). Built up over time from interactions; editable via update_researcher_profile. Returns fields + a formatted context_string for injection.

_No parameters._

```python
get_researcher_profile()
```

#### `update_researcher_profile`

Update the researcher profile. Strings replace; list fields (expertise, preferred_models) append; dict fields (tools_familiarity) merge. Call when the user states or you infer something durable about them.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | any | — |  |
| `institution` | any | — |  |
| `role` | any | — |  |
| `domain` | any | — |  |
| `research_focus` | any | — |  |
| `expertise` | any | — |  |
| `preferred_models` | any | — |  |
| `tools_familiarity` | any | — |  |
| `communication_style` | any | — |  |
| `active_project` | any | — |  |

```python
update_researcher_profile(name='...', institution='...')
```

### HTML Preview

#### `preview_focus_cell`

Scroll the HTML Preview to a specific cell and highlight it.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `module_id` | string | yes |  |
| `cell_id` | string | yes |  |

```python
preview_focus_cell(module_id='...', cell_id='...')
```

#### `preview_get_pending_changes`

Return the batched queue of pending user comments + text edits for a module (from ~/.aihydro/comments/<id>.json). Process them in one turn, then preview_address_comment each. status: open (default) | awaiting_review | addressed | all.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `module_id` | string | yes |  |
| `status` | string | — |  |

```python
preview_get_pending_changes(module_id='...')
```

#### `preview_get_state`

HTML Preview session snapshot: manifest, cell registry (IDs + last-run status), recent_errors. module_id defaults to most-recently-updated.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `module_id` | any | — |  |

```python
preview_get_state(module_id='...')
```

#### `preview_list_modules`

List the module IDs that currently have an open preview session.

_No parameters._

```python
preview_list_modules()
```

#### `preview_recent_events`

Recent PreviewEvent records (cell runs/errors, user comments, iframe interactions). Poll with since_seq for new-only. kind_filter examples: cell.error | user.comment | cell.run.completed. limit cap 200.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `module_id` | any | — |  |
| `since_seq` | integer | — |  |
| `kind_filter` | any | — |  |
| `limit` | integer | — |  |

```python
preview_recent_events(module_id='...', since_seq=0)
```

#### `show_html_preview`

Open an HTML file in the AI-Hydro HTML Preview panel (executes any .aihydro-cell Python cells inline). Use after writing a module via the interactive-module-builder skill. file_path must be absolute.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `file_path` | string | yes |  |
| `title` | any | — |  |

```python
show_html_preview(file_path='...')
```

### Projects

#### `add_session_to_project`

Link a session to a project. Session need not exist yet (pre-registration OK). Returns the updated session list.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_name` | string | yes |  |
| `session_id` | string | yes |  |

```python
add_session_to_project(project_name='...', session_id='01646500')
```

#### `get_project_summary`

Overview of a project: metadata, session summaries (with computed/pending slots), recent journal entries, literature index status.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `project_name` | string | yes |  |

```python
get_project_summary(project_name='...')
```

#### `start_project`

Create or resume a named research project — top-level unit spanning multiple sessions + a literature folder + experiment journal. Idempotent. name: directory-safe slug (no spaces). Sets the project as active in the researcher profile.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | yes |  |
| `description` | string | — |  |
| `topics` | any | — |  |

```python
start_project(name='...')
```

### Session Management

#### `archive_session`

Freeze the session: move current interpretations + notes to a 'Historical' section in research.md. Use when concluding a study phase. Archived sessions stay readable and exportable.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |

```python
archive_session(session_id='01646500')
```

#### `clear_session`

Clear cached results to force recompute. Notes/workspace_dir/site_name/ interpretation are always preserved. slots: subset of [watershed, streamflow, signatures, geomorphic, camels, forcing, twi, cn, model] (omit for all data slots).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `slots` | any | — |  |

```python
clear_session(session_id='01646500', slots='...')
```

#### `get_session_health`

Audit a session for identity drift, workspace problems, and consistency gaps. Returns warnings keyed by code so the agent can fix issues before they propagate into research.md, exports, or downstream tools.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |

```python
get_session_health(session_id='01646500')
```

#### `get_session_raw_state`

Phase 1 of interpretation: return raw computed state for the LLM to read (large arrays summarised). Follow with write_research_interpretation to author + persist the prose synthesis.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |

```python
get_session_raw_state(session_id='01646500')
```

#### `get_session_summary` · :material-fire: hot

Return computed/pending slots for the session + notes + interpretation. session_id is optional — auto-resolved from the active chat when omitted.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |

```python
get_session_summary(session_id='01646500')
```

#### `merge_session_shards`

Consolidate sub-agent shard files into the main session (notes, citations, slots). Shard files are deleted after merge. shard_ids: subset or omit to merge all available shards.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `shard_ids` | any | — |  |

```python
merge_session_shards(session_id='01646500', shard_ids='...')
```

#### `start_session` · :material-fire: hot

Start or resume a research session (persistent memory for a study). session_id: any string (slug, USGS gauge id, UUID); auto-generated if omitted. workspace_dir: pass once, remembered for all future calls — enables auto-save of tool outputs there. shard_id: for sub-agent forks.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `session_id` | any | — |  |
| `shard_id` | any | — |  |
| `workspace_dir` | any | — |  |

```python
start_session(session_id='01646500', shard_id='...')
```

### Skills

#### `list_skills`

List installed workflow skills (playbooks). See SKILL DISCOVERY in the system prompt for the mandatory pre-flight protocol. ``domain`` filters to one of: frequency-analysis, baseflow, modelling, interpretation, composition, teaching, general.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `domain` | any | — |  |
| `workspace_dir` | any | — |  |

```python
list_skills(domain='...', workspace_dir='...')
```

#### `load_skill`

Load a skill's full SKILL.md (frontmatter + body). The skill's steps and format contracts are binding — see SKILL DISCOVERY in the system prompt. name: exact match from list_skills().

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | yes |  |
| `workspace_dir` | any | — |  |

```python
load_skill(name='...')
```

#### `save_skill`

Persist a new SKILL.md (Agent Skills open format) to ~/.aihydro/skills/agent-created/. Use after completing a novel workflow worth reusing. name will be slugified; content is the markdown body.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | yes |  |
| `description` | string | yes |  |
| `content` | string | yes |  |
| `domain` | string | — |  |
| `when_to_use` | string | — |  |
| `tags` | any | — |  |
| `tools_used` | any | — |  |

```python
save_skill(name='...', description='...', content='...')
```

```python
fetch_streamflow_data(session_id='01646500', gauge_id='01646500')
```
